title: Cochlidium minus
intro: Cochlidium minus is a species of epiphytic fern native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. Traditionally, this plant has been used in folk medicine for its purported health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in the treatment of various conditions such as fever, bronchitis, and gastrointestinal issues. The bioactive constituents of Cochlidium minus have been identified to include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Cochlidium minus is often prepared as a decoction or tea, and is also used in the form of tinctures and infusions to harness its health benefits.
taxonomy: Cochlidium minus is a species of epiphytic fern in the family Polypodiaceae. It is a type of air plant, which is a term used to describe plants that grow on other plants without harming them. Cochlidium minus is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. This species of fern is often grown indoors as an ornamental plant due to its delicate appearance. The classification of Cochlidium minus as a species of fern makes it a part of the larger kingdom Plantae.
